Package com.saperion.ngc.iform.xml

Class OutlinerType

java.lang.Object
com.saperion.ngc.iform.xml.OutlinerType
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class OutlinerType extends Object implements Serializable

Java class for OutlinerType complex type.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class. 

 <complexType name="OutlinerType">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="font" type="{http://www.saperion.com/NGXMLSchema}fontType" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="pos" type="{http://www.saperion.com/NGXMLSchema}posType"/>
         <element name="cntLevels" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}unsignedByte"/>
         <element name="oStyle" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}unsignedByte"/>
         <element name="rootPict" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}base64Binary"/>
         <element name="rootName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="rootDisabled" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean"/>
         <element name="hideHeader" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean"/>
         <element name="hideEmpty" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}boolean"/>
         <element name="level" type="{http://www.saperion.com/NGXMLSchema}levelType" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getLevel

      public List<LevelType> getLevel()
      Gets the value of the level property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the JAXB object. This is why there is not a set method for the level property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

          getLevel().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list LevelType
